mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate reduces discount rates and eases financial conditions, lifting equity valuations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days while inflation stays contained.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not higher when FEDFUNDS is falling than when it is flat or rising, or if the sign turns negative during periods of low and stable CPIAUCSL inflation.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in UNRATE above recent levels signals weakening household income and demand, which compresses revenue expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment rises are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC stays above zero after sign inversion.

mechanism_narrative: Rising RSAFS alongside rising PAYEMS indicates broadening final demand and payroll-supported consumption, which improves near-term earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns positive over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition RSAFS up and PAYEMS up does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal is not stronger than chance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO reflects manufacturing and industrial contraction that weakens cyclical earnings and raises recession risk, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if two-month declines in INDPRO are not followed by lower 60-day SPX returns than non-decline periods, or if the signal loses predictive power in walk-forward tests after accounting for revision risk.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts rebound after a prior slump signals improving construction demand, employment, and credit transmission, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if housing-starts rebounds are not associated with above-average 60-day SPX gains, or if the effect disappears when HOUST is not recovering from a trough.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is near the zero lower bound and CPIAUCSL inflation is rising, the market reads policy as constrained but growth-supportive, so equities should deliver positive short-horizon returns as nominal activity expectations improve.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-rate/high-inflation condition fails to produce positive 5-day SPX returns on average, or if the interaction term is not significant while the single-series effects explain the move.

mechanism_narrative: A payrolls surprise with PAYEMS rising while UNRATE falls indicates labor-market tightening and stronger consumption capacity, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS up month-over-month and UNRATE down month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous payroll gains and unemployment declines do not outperform either labor series alone, or if triggered instances fail to show a positive median 20-day SPX return.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronous slowdown in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates weakening aggregate demand, lowering expected sales growth and compressing equity multiples, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth below 1% annualized and RSAFS growth below 0%
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous weak GDP and sales readings are not followed by underperformance in 60-day SPX returns, or if the joint condition does not add predictive power beyond either proxy separately.
